title: PUE Calculation Algorithm
description: Algorithm to calculate Power Usage Effectiveness of a data center
code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_PUE
INPUT: TotalFacilityEnergy, ITEquipmentEnergy
OUTPUT: PUE_Value, EfficiencyRating

BEGIN
 // Validate inputs
 IF ITEquipmentEnergy <= 0 THEN
 RETURN "Error: IT energy must be positive"
 ENDIF
 
 // Calculate PUE
 PUE_Value = TotalFacilityEnergy / ITEquipmentEnergy
 
 // Determine efficiency rating
 IF PUE_Value <= 1.2 THEN
 EfficiencyRating = "Very Efficient"
 ELSE IF PUE_Value <= 1.5 THEN
 EfficiencyRating = "Efficient"
 ELSE IF PUE_Value <= 2.0 THEN
 EfficiencyRating = "Average"
 ELSE
 EfficiencyRating = "Inefficient"
 ENDIF
 
 // Calculate DCiE
 DCiE = (ITEquipmentEnergy / TotalFacilityEnergy) * 100
 
 RETURN PUE_Value, DCiE, EfficiencyRating
END
explanation: This algorithm calculates PUE by dividing total facility energy by IT equipment energy. It also calculates DCiE (the inverse percentage) and provides an efficiency rating based on industry standards.

title: Simple Virtual Machine Consolidation Algorithm
description: Algorithm to consolidate workloads onto fewer servers
code:
ALGORITHM VM_Consolidation
INPUT: ListOfVMs, ListOfPhysicalServers
OUTPUT: VM_to_Server_Mapping

BEGIN
 // Sort servers by capacity (descending)
 Sort(ListOfPhysicalServers, by=AvailableCapacity DESC)
 
 // Sort VMs by resource requirements
 Sort(ListOfVMs, by=ResourceRequirement DESC)
 
 FOR each VM in ListOfVMs DO
 assigned = FALSE
 
 FOR each Server in ListOfPhysicalServers DO
 IF Server.CanAccommodate(VM) THEN
 Server.AssignVM(VM)
 assigned = TRUE
 BREAK
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 IF NOT assigned THEN
 RETURN "Error: Cannot fit all VMs"
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 RETURN VM_to_Server_Mapping
END
explanation: This algorithm assigns virtual machines to physical servers to minimize the number of servers used. It places larger VMs first on servers with more capacity, optimizing resource utilization.
